channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-1195] ll_sai_put()) ASSERTION(thread_is_stopped(&sai->sai_agl_thread)) failed
item.description: With a mounted file system I power down the MDS server to test the IR functionality. The client I was one which was running a IOR job from the oops while waiting to recovery.
item.environment: Lustre 2.1.56 cray clients with RHEL6 back end servers running Lustre 2.1.56

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
Integrated in lustre-dev » x86_64,client,el6,inkernel #340
LU-1195 llite: error handling for statahead/AGL start failure (Revision f665b5f7691740cbdae6c96c5d959dfb7659f679)

Result = SUCCESS

Files :

  • lustre/llite/statahead.c
Sure. This is something we should do for the IR test shot as well.

Hi James, can you please turn panic_on_lbug off so that you can collect some log in case it runs into problem?

This is my Lustre config file:

[root@mercury tests]# cat /etc/modprobe.d/Lustre
#options obdclass lu_cache_percent=1
options libcfs libcfs_panic_on_lbug=0

or you can run `lctl set_param panic_on_lbug=0' on each node if there is no disks on client side.

I'm in the process of testing with the patch to see if this is the case. The failure of the cfs_create_thread might be from the rhel5 corruption but please apply this patch if it has no regression. The test failure did expose a real defect for error handing for a failing agl thread.

James

Does this problem still exist or was it just a side-effect of the mismatch with the RHEL5 version as mentioned in LU-1135?

Peter
Strange that the cfs_create_thread failed client side since the problems were happening server server due to LU-1135. This addes another clue to LU-1135

I just changed the description of this bug. This is because there is no any evidence that this is related to IR

So let's ask them to apply nasf's patch and see what's going on next. Please let me know if you have any questions.

This is the patch LBUG related with statahead/AGL start failure:

http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,2268

In fact, the AGL was not started, some unknown reason caused cfs_create_thread() failed, and there is defect for error handling under the case of failing to statahead/agl thread, then LBUG() was triggered here. I will make patch to fix it.

As for why cfs_create_thread() failed to start some kernel threads, it is out of statahead/agl scope. So my patch cannot resolve the possible potential IR issues.

I'm wondering why this client was evicted by OST-0002 and OST-0006 while MDT was restarted. The assertion looks related to AGL so I add nasf into watch list.

Can you please collect log on the client side?

Jinshan

Could you comment on this one please?

Thanks

Peter

....
